title: Why .env files exist
problem: A developer hardcoded an API key in a Python file and pushed it to a public GitHub repo. Within 4 hours the account was charged $3,000. Explain the full attack chain, then describe the correct way to handle secrets in a production Python application.
hints: GitHub is indexed. Bots scan for hardcoded keys within minutes.
The fix isn't just 'use .env' — you also need to understand what not to commit.
Three layers: environment variables, .gitignore, and rotating the leaked key immediately.
solution: Attack chain: developer pushes file → bots scan new commits for key patterns → key found within minutes → used to make expensive API calls. Fix: (1) read secrets only from os.getenv(), never literals; (2) add .env to .gitignore; (3) if a key is ever exposed, rotate it immediately — removing the commit is not enough as scrapers may have already captured it.
